pixfrog
Flash Features Docs About GitHub ↗
ESP32-P4 firmware

Flash your ESP32-P4
right from the browser

pixfrog is a high-density LED-pixel controller firmware. Plug the board in over USB and install the latest build in seconds — no IDE, no toolchain, no command line.

Use desktop Chrome or Edge to flash Open over HTTPS to flash 📦 Releases 📖 Documentation

Works in desktop Chrome & Edge (Web Serial). The chip is erased and reflashed — settings stored in NVS are reset.

Three steps to blinking pixels

From a bare board to running firmware without installing anything.

Connect

Plug the ESP32-P4 into a USB port and click Connect & Install above. Pick the board's serial port.

Install

The flasher erases the chip and writes the latest firmware image. It takes about a minute.

Configure

Use the on-board OLED + rotary encoder to set protocol, universe, channels and network — all persisted to NVS.

What's inside

Built natively on ESP-IDF for the ESP32-P4, tuned for deterministic pixel timing.

🎚️

8 LED protocols

WS2815, WS2812B, WS2811, SK6812, WS2814, APA102, SK9822 and LPD8806 — with per-channel color order.

🌐

Art-Net ingest

UDP Art-Net over Ethernet with universe mapping, ArtPollReply and a lwIP stack tuned for high packet rates.

⚡

LCD_CAM parallel out

The ESP32-P4 LCD_CAM peripheral clocks 8 parallel channels out of PSRAM frame buffers via GDMA for rock-steady refresh.

🖥️

On-device UI

SSD1306 OLED menu driven by a seesaw rotary encoder — live FPS, IP, link state and full configuration, no laptop needed.

💾

Persistent config

Every setting is stored in NVS and survives reboots, with a loud warning on the home screen if flash goes bad.

🧩

Octal PSRAM

200 MHz octal PSRAM holds the double-buffered frames while DMA-capable SRAM keeps the output path fast.

Read the architecture →

Prefer the command line?

Every release ships raw binaries you can flash with esptool or idf.py.

💡
Flash pixfrog-merged.bin at offset 0x0, or write the individual parts at their offsets: bootloader 0x2000, partition table 0x8000, app 0x10000.
pixfrog-merged.bin bootloader.bin partition-table.bin pixfrog.bin
🐸 pixfrog · latest · MIT licensed Source · Docs · Releases · built with esp-web-tools
